surprise.caption: What the sleepy puppy loves most after lunch is a…
surprise.explanation: The puppy is curled up asleep — he is taking a NAP. Drag the three circled letters into the answer bar.
surprise.explanation_ru: «NAP» — короткий дневной сон, «вздремнуть». Щенок свернулся калачиком после обеда — он прилёг вздремнуть.

cartoon.prompt: Cheerful flat-color cartoon illustration for a casual mobile puzzle game, single panel gag comic, bold clean outlines, soft warm palette, simple background. A tiny happy puppy curled up asleep in a sunny dog bed, big Z's floating above, one eye peeking. Speech bubble: "Zzz…". no title, no logo, no header text, no watermark

surprise.caption: The glazier fixed the broken window and now the house has a brand new…
surprise.explanation: A sheet of window glass is a PANE. The cartoon shows a fresh pane of glass in the frame.
surprise.explanation_ru: «PANE» — оконное стекло (window pane). Стекольщик заменил разбитое окно — в доме новое стекло.

cartoon.prompt: Cheerful flat-color cartoon illustration for a casual mobile puzzle game, single panel gag comic, bold clean outlines, soft warm palette, simple background. A friendly workman installing a sparkling clean square of window glass into a house frame, thumbs up. Speech bubble: "Good as new!". no title, no logo, no header text, no watermark

surprise.caption: When the two runners bumped into each other at the horse races, it turned into a track…
surprise.explanation: A running competition is a track MEET — and “meet” sounds just like “meat”, which is why one runner is holding a steak.
surprise.explanation_ru: «MEET» — спортивный сбор/забег (track meet). Звучит как «meat» (мясо) — поэтому один из бегунов размахивает стейком. Игра на созвучии meet/meat.

cartoon.prompt: Cheerful flat-color cartoon illustration for a casual mobile puzzle game, single panel gag comic, bold clean outlines, soft warm palette, simple background. Two cartoon joggers in running gear surprised to bump into each other at a horse racing track, one comically waving a steak. Speech bubble: "Fancy meeting you here!". no title, no logo, no header text, no watermark

surprise.caption: When the charter pilot’s son took over the family flying business, it became an…
surprise.explanation: The son who inherits is the HEIR, and “HEIR FORCE” sounds like “air force” — a flying business run by the heir.
surprise.explanation_ru: «HEIR» — наследник. «HEIR FORCE» произносится как «air force» (военно-воздушные силы). Сын унаследовал авиабизнес — вышли «военно-наследственные силы».

cartoon.prompt: Cheerful flat-color cartoon illustration for a casual mobile puzzle game, single panel gag comic, bold clean outlines, soft warm palette, simple background. A proud young pilot in a tiny propeller plane saluting, wearing a crown, an older pilot handing him the keys. Speech bubble: "It's all yours, son!". no title, no logo, no header text, no watermark

surprise.caption: The hat shop’s sales dropped because customers thought the styles were all…
surprise.explanation: “OLD HAT” is an idiom meaning outdated — fitting for a hat shop whose designs are behind the times.
surprise.explanation_ru: «OLD HAT» — английская идиома «устаревшее, приевшееся, вчерашний день». Шляпный магазин прогорел, потому что фасоны были старомодными — буквально «старые шляпы».

cartoon.prompt: Cheerful flat-color cartoon illustration for a casual mobile puzzle game, single panel gag comic, bold clean outlines, soft warm palette, simple background. A dusty old-fashioned hat shop with cobwebbed bonnets, a bored shopkeeper, one customer yawning at the styles. Speech bubble: "Seen it all before!". no title, no logo, no header text, no watermark

surprise.caption: What a wooden boardwalk feels when far too many tourists crowd onto it at once…
surprise.explanation: A PIER is the boardwalk over water; “PIER PRESSURE” sounds like “peer pressure” — here it's the literal weight of the crowd.
surprise.explanation_ru: «PIER» — пирс, помост над водой. «PIER PRESSURE» звучит как «peer pressure» (давление сверстников), но здесь это буквальный вес толпы туристов, давящий на пирс.

cartoon.prompt: Cheerful flat-color cartoon illustration for a casual mobile puzzle game, single panel gag comic, bold clean outlines, soft warm palette, simple background. A crowded seaside wooden pier bending under a huge crowd of happy tourists, planks creaking, one worried crab below. Speech bubble: "Room for one more?". no title, no logo, no header text, no watermark

surprise.caption: When the humble gentleman unexpectedly won the distinguished-gentleman contest, everyone called it a…
surprise.explanation: A knighted man is a SIR, and “SIR PRIZE” is said like “surprise” — the modest gentleman was shocked to win.
surprise.explanation_ru: «SIR» — сэр, титул. «SIR PRIZE» произносится как «surprise» (сюрприз). Скромный джентльмен неожиданно выиграл приз — вот вам и «сэр-приз».

cartoon.prompt: Cheerful flat-color cartoon illustration for a casual mobile puzzle game, single panel gag comic, bold clean outlines, soft warm palette, simple background. A blushing gentleman with a monocle and top hat, shocked to be handed a golden trophy on a red-carpet stage, confetti falling. Speech bubble: "Me? Really?!". no title, no logo, no header text, no watermark

surprise.caption: What the flashy TV host shot with his camera while on safari turned out to be a…
surprise.explanation: Wild safari animals are called “game”, and a “GAME SHOW” is a TV programme — the host “shot” the game with a camera, not a rifle.
surprise.explanation_ru: «GAME» — это и дичь на сафари, и «GAME SHOW» (телевикторина). Гламурный ведущий «подстрелил» дичь камерой, а не ружьём — получилось телешоу.

cartoon.prompt: Cheerful flat-color cartoon illustration for a casual mobile puzzle game, single panel gag comic, bold clean outlines, soft warm palette, simple background. A sparkly TV game-show host in a safari hat filming friendly zebras and giraffes with a camera on the savanna, applause sign glowing. Speech bubble: "And our next contestant!". no title, no logo, no header text, no watermark

surprise.caption: The buzzing flies kept landing on the movie set and every one of them was hoping to pass the…
surprise.explanation: A SCREEN TEST is an audition for film; the flies want to be on screen — but a window “screen” also keeps flies out, the double joke.
surprise.explanation_ru: «SCREEN TEST» — кинопроба (проба на экран). Мухи мечтают попасть на экран, но оконная «screen» (москитная сетка) — как раз то, что их не пускает. Двойная игра слов на screen.

cartoon.prompt: Cheerful flat-color cartoon illustration for a casual mobile puzzle game, single panel gag comic, bold clean outlines, soft warm palette, simple background. Movie set with a director's chair and clapperboard, cartoon flies wearing sunglasses buzzing hopefully toward a movie camera and a window screen. Speech bubble: "We're ready for our close-up!". no title, no logo, no header text, no watermark

surprise.caption: When the glazier’s son finally replaced the cracked window that had bothered everyone, the doctor felt sweet…
surprise.explanation: A window PANE, said aloud, is “pain” — so “PANE RELIEF” is “pain relief”. The doctor is relieved the painful (pane-ful) window is fixed.
surprise.explanation_ru: «PANE» (оконное стекло) звучит как «pain» (боль). «PANE RELIEF» = «pain relief» (обезболивание). Доктор вздохнул с облегчением, когда починили мучившее всех окно — «стекло-болеутоляющее».

cartoon.prompt: Cheerful flat-color cartoon illustration for a casual mobile puzzle game, single panel gag comic, bold clean outlines, soft warm palette, simple background. A weary doctor sighing with relief in a waiting room as sunlight streams through a freshly repaired window, an aspirin bottle on the desk untouched. Speech bubble: "Ahh, much better.". no title, no logo, no header text, no watermark
